title: Human Approval for AI Actions
description:
## Put a Human in the Loop

An AI workflow can propose **high-impact, irreversible actions** (for example, issuing a customer refund or modifying production infrastructure). Governance requires that such an action **pauses for explicit human approval** before it executes, and the workflow must resume automatically once a person approves or rejects.

Which AWS service is best suited to orchestrate this human-in-the-loop approval step?

options.text: AWS Step Functions, using a task that waits on a callback (task token) until a human approves or rejects
Amazon SNS, by emailing the team and letting the action proceed immediately
An EventBridge rule that triggers the action as soon as the AI proposes it
A Lambda function that sleeps for 24 hours and then runs the action regardless

explanation: AWS Step Functions can model a human-in-the-loop gate with the wait-for-callback pattern: the state machine pauses on a task token and only resumes when an external approval (or rejection) is sent back, after which it branches accordingly. SNS (b) and EventBridge (c) only notify or trigger — they don't pause and wait for a decision. A Lambda that sleeps then proceeds (d) is not real oversight; it executes the action without an actual approval and is bounded by Lambda's timeout.

hints.title: Pause, wait, resume
hints.body: You need an orchestrator that can suspend execution until an external human decision arrives, then continue — that's a workflow/state-machine capability.
